page: Amazon Web Services m7i.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 105 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. Network connectivity is delivered via a 12 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific processing tasks. The instance demonstrates strong single-core and multi-core Geekbench scores, alongside strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression, image processing, and text processing. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, it achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 413.98 tokens per second and strong prompt processing speeds. For larger workloads, such as a medium 7B model, prompt processing remains strong, while text generation and larger 70B model inference perform at average levels. Standard computational benchmarks, including stress-ng, Passmark CPU, and bzip3 decompression, consistently register in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i.8xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads. The combination of DDR5 memory and Intel Xeon processors with advanced instruction sets, such as AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions, delivers high resource density without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means storage-heavy workloads will incur additional network storage costs. This instance is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz and 128.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression, image processing, text processing, and small-model LLM text generation, alongside average performance in standard multi-core stress tests and database operations. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and advanced instruction support make it a cost-efficient choice for general-purpose enterprise applications, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance for web serving and compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced Intel Xeon processors for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 105 MB L3 cache
Strong performance in image processing, text processing, and web serving
Top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale LLM inference
No local storage or GPU, utilizing a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suitable for software compilation, database operations, and general-purpose workloads
